# Generated by iptables-save v1.8.7 on Mon May 18 08:00:00 2026
*filter
:INPUT DROP [0:0]
:FORWARD DROP [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]

# ─── INPUT chain rules ───────────────────────────────────────

# Allow established and related connections
-A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT

# Drop invalid packets
-A INPUT -m state --state INVALID -j DROP

# Allow loopback interface
-A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT

# Allow ICMP (ping) — rate limited
-A INPUT -p icmp --icmp-type echo-request -m limit --limit 1/s -j ACCEPT

# Allow SSH from internal network only (10.0.0.0/8)
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow HTTP from anywhere
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow HTTPS from anywhere
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 443 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow NTP (outbound NTP responses)
-A INPUT -p udp --sport 123 -j ACCEPT

# Allow DNS responses
-A INPUT -p udp --sport 53 -j ACCEPT

# ─── PROBLEM 1: MySQL port 3306 open to ALL sources (CRITICAL) ───
# BUG: Should be restricted to 10.0.0.0/8 (internal only)
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 3306 -s 0.0.0.0/0 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow PostgreSQL from internal only (correctly restricted)
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 5432 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow Redis from internal only (correctly restricted)
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 6379 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow monitoring (Prometheus node exporter) from monitoring subnet
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9100 -s 10.0.1.0/24 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow application API port from internal
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8443 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# ─── PROBLEM 2: SSH port 22 also open to ALL — duplicate/override risk (RISKY) ───
# BUG: This rule appears AFTER the restrictive rule but creates confusion
# and will ACCEPT SSH from any source if rule order changes
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -s 0.0.0.0/0 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow FTP passive ports from internal only
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 21 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 20 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 60000:65000 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow Zabbix agent from monitoring server
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 10050 -s 10.0.1.5 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow SNMP from network management
-A INPUT -p udp --dport 161 -s 10.0.2.0/24 -j ACCEPT

# ─── PROBLEM 3: Dev server port 8080 exposed to internet (RISKY) ───
# BUG: Development portal should NOT be accessible from 0.0.0.0/0
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -s 0.0.0.0/0 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# Allow VPN (OpenVPN)
-A INPUT -p udp --dport 1194 -s 0.0.0.0/0 -j ACCEPT

# Allow application port 8443 staging from internal
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8444 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -m state --state NEW -j ACCEPT

# ─── PROBLEM 4: Overly permissive ACCEPT rule (CRITICAL if activated) ───
# DANGER: This line, if uncommented, would ACCEPT ALL traffic before DROP rules
# It is currently commented out but represents a critical misconfiguration risk
#-A INPUT -j ACCEPT

# Final catch-all DROP rule (default policy reinforcement)
-A INPUT -j DROP

# ─── OUTPUT chain rules ───────────────────────────────────────

# Allow all outbound traffic
-A OUTPUT -j ACCEPT

# ─── FORWARD chain rules ──────────────────────────────────────

# This host is not a router — drop all forwarded traffic
-A FORWARD -j DROP

COMMIT
# Completed on Mon May 18 08:00:00 2026
